description: Cross-cutting eval: skills must detect codegraph availability from the tea-rags prime digest and route to non-codegraph fallbacks (or honest 'unavailable') when codegraph is OFF, while still using codegraph tools when ON. Bead tea-rags-mcp-3hkuy.
detection_signal: prime digest '## Enrichment' line 'codegraph.symbols: file healthy, chunk healthy' present => codegraph ON; absent => codegraph OFF (graph tools not registered, per src/mcp/tools/codegraph.ts:128).

results.harness: honest-v2 (raw prime block, per-ENV tool list, graph tools ABSENT in ENV-A; contaminated-v1 archived under workspace/iteration-1/contaminated-v1)
results.note: F2/F3 dangerous false-claims NOT reproduced by either arm — registration gating makes codegraph absence self-evident to an Opus-class model. Fix value = instruction correctness ('returns empty' was factually wrong) + weaker-model robustness.

evals.name: cycle-off-no-false-dag
evals.prompt: [prime digest: ## Enrichment shows only 'git: file healthy, chunk healthy' — NO codegraph.symbols line] Are there any circular dependencies in src/core/domains/ingest?
evals.expected: Detect codegraph OFF from prime (no codegraph.symbols enrichment) BEFORE calling find_cycles. State cycle detection requires codegraph / is unavailable. MUST NOT call find_cycles and report 'no cycles, it's a DAG'.
evals.failure_mode: Calls find_cycles, gets empty, falsely reports 'no circular dependencies / it's a DAG'.

assertions.text: detects codegraph OFF from the prime digest before any graph call
does NOT claim 'no cycles' / 'it is a DAG'
states cycle detection is unavailable without codegraph (no content-search substitute claimed as cycle truth)

evals.name: sysdebug-off-no-drop-hypothesis
evals.prompt: [prime digest: NO codegraph.symbols enrichment line] Debugging a checkout failure. I think the request handler reaches PaymentService#charge. A trace_path from the handler to PaymentService#charge came back empty. Is that hypothesis dead?
evals.expected: Recognize the empty result is because codegraph is OFF (per prime), NOT a proof the path is structurally false. Keep the hypothesis; fall back to bug-hunt flat bugFixRate suspects / manual reasoning.
evals.failure_mode: Treats empty trace_path as 'path structurally false' and drops the hypothesis (the systematic-debugging:213 anti-pattern firing wrongly when codegraph is off).

assertions.text: attributes the empty trace_path to codegraph being OFF, not to a missing call path
does NOT drop the hypothesis as structurally false
routes to a non-codegraph fallback (bugFixRate suspects / manual call reasoning)

evals.name: usage-off-proactive-fallback
evals.prompt: [prime digest: NO codegraph.symbols enrichment line] Who calls AppConfig#load?
evals.expected: Detect codegraph OFF from prime FIRST, then route directly to hybrid_search / find_symbol (name-match, no edge truth) and state the precision caveat. Do not lead with get_callers.
evals.failure_mode: Calls get_callers as primary, waits for empty result, then reacts (reactive not proactive).

assertions.text: checks prime for codegraph availability before choosing the tool
primary tool is hybrid_search / find_symbol, NOT get_callers
states the fallback has no edge truth (name-match precision caveat)

evals.name: tracepath-off-fallback-not-codegraph
evals.prompt: [prime digest: NO codegraph.symbols enrichment line] Show me the call path from Router#dispatch to Db#query.
evals.expected: Detect OFF; trace_path AND get_callees are both unavailable. Fall back to semantic_search / hybrid_search + manual reasoning and say there is no static path tool. MUST NOT propose get_callees breadth-first as the fallback (it is also codegraph).
evals.failure_mode: Falls back to get_callees breadth-first (the broken search-cascade fallback row) — itself codegraph-dependent, also empty.

assertions.text: detects codegraph OFF from prime
does NOT propose get_callees / get_callers as the fallback
fallback is a non-codegraph route (semantic/hybrid + manual) with an honest 'no static path' caveat

evals.name: architecture-off-no-false-backbone
evals.prompt: [prime digest: NO codegraph.symbols enrichment line] What's the architectural backbone of domains/trajectory?
evals.expected: Detect OFF; architecturalHub has no codegraph signals so it degrades to similarity. Say structural centrality (fan-in hubs) is unavailable; offer git imports/churn or relevance instead. MUST NOT present the top similarity hit as 'the architectural centre'.
evals.failure_mode: Runs rerank=architecturalHub and narrates structural-hub truth from a preset that has no codegraph data.

assertions.text: detects codegraph OFF and states fan-in/hub centrality is unavailable
does NOT present a similarity top hit as the structural backbone
offers a non-codegraph substitute (git imports/churn or relevance) labeled as such

evals.name: bughunt-off-flat-suspects-only
evals.prompt: [prime digest: NO codegraph.symbols enrichment line] Debug why checkout fails — trace from the controller down to the charge call to find the buggy hop.
evals.expected: Detect OFF; the trace_path danger-chain is unavailable. Use the flat bugFixRate / churn suspect list only and say the call-chain ranking is unavailable without codegraph.
evals.failure_mode: Instructs trace_path(rerank=bugHunt) as if available; no codegraph-off guard.

assertions.text: detects codegraph OFF from prime
does NOT rely on trace_path for the causal chain
falls back to flat bugFixRate/churn suspects with an explicit 'chain ranking unavailable' note

evals.name: control-cycle-on
evals.prompt: [prime digest: ## Enrichment shows 'codegraph.symbols: file healthy, chunk healthy'] Are there any circular dependencies in domains/ingest?
evals.expected: Codegraph ON: route to find_cycles scope=file pathPattern='**/domains/ingest/**'. Empty result = honest 'no cycles, it is a DAG'.
evals.failure_mode: Over-triggers the fallback and avoids find_cycles even though codegraph is on.

assertions.text: uses find_cycles (codegraph) as the primary tool
does NOT route to a content-search fallback when codegraph is ON

evals.name: control-usage-on
evals.prompt: [prime digest: 'codegraph.symbols: file healthy, chunk healthy' present] Who calls AppConfig#load?
evals.expected: Codegraph ON: find_symbol to resolve exact id, then get_callers symbolId=<id>.
evals.failure_mode: Falls back to hybrid_search despite codegraph being on (false negative on detection).

assertions.text: resolves the symbol id then uses get_callers (codegraph)
does NOT downgrade to hybrid_search-only when codegraph is ON

evals.name: control-tracepath-on
evals.prompt: [prime digest: 'codegraph.symbols: file healthy, chunk healthy' present] Debugging: trace the path from the request handler to PaymentService#charge to find the riskiest hop.
evals.expected: Codegraph ON: use trace_path(from, to, rerank='bugHunt') for the danger-ranked chain.
evals.failure_mode: Avoids trace_path even though codegraph is on.

assertions.text: uses trace_path (codegraph) for the danger-ranked chain
does NOT claim codegraph is unavailable when prime shows it ON

evals.name: edge-on-genuine-empty-is-real-dag
evals.prompt: [prime digest: 'codegraph.symbols: file healthy, chunk healthy' present] Any circular dependencies in src/core/utils? (the module is genuinely acyclic)
evals.expected: Codegraph ON: find_cycles returns empty. Because codegraph IS on (per prime), empty is the TRUE answer: 'no cycles, it is a DAG'. MUST NOT misattribute the empty result to codegraph being off.
evals.failure_mode: Sees empty find_cycles and wrongly says 'codegraph unavailable' instead of 'no cycles'.

assertions.text: uses prime to confirm codegraph is ON, so empty = genuine DAG
reports 'no cycles / DAG' and does NOT claim codegraph is unavailable

evals.name: edge-nonenglish-off
evals.prompt: [prime digest: NO codegraph.symbols enrichment line] найди циклические зависимости в domains/language
evals.expected: Translate intent (cycles), detect codegraph OFF from prime, state cycle detection unavailable without codegraph. Same behavior as E1 regardless of input language.
evals.failure_mode: Non-English bypasses the guard; calls find_cycles and falsely reports no cycles.

assertions.text: classifies the Russian prompt as a cycle/codegraph intent
detects codegraph OFF and does NOT falsely report 'no cycles'

evals.name: subagent-explore-entrypoint-off
evals.prompt: [subagent: explore] [prime digest: NO codegraph.symbols enrichment line] Explore /Users/artk0re/Dev/Tools/tea-rags-mcp/src/core/domains/ingest — where does the indexing flow start (entry points)?
evals.expected: As a subagent with limited context, still detect codegraph OFF from the provided prime, recognize entryPoint preset degrades to relevance, use relevance + chunkSize heuristic with an explicit caveat. No false 'this is THE entry point' from fan-graph signals.
evals.failure_mode: Subagent ignores prime, runs rerank=entryPoint, narrates fan-out-driver truth that the preset cannot back without codegraph.

assertions.text: subagent detects codegraph OFF from the provided prime digest
uses relevance + chunkSize heuristic with a caveat, not fan-graph hub/driver claims

evals.name: subagent-taskagent-usage-on
evals.prompt: [subagent: task-agent] [prime digest: 'codegraph.symbols: file healthy, chunk healthy' present] For the refactor task, find all callers of OrderService#submit (file: app/services/order_service.rb).
evals.expected: Subagent with codegraph ON: find_symbol to resolve id, then get_callers. Uses codegraph edge truth.
evals.failure_mode: Subagent defaults to ripgrep/hybrid despite codegraph being on.

assertions.text: resolves symbol id then uses get_callers (codegraph) for exact callers
does NOT default to ripgrep/grep for a who-calls query when codegraph is ON
